flashcards.back: A system that connects multiple processors, memory modules, and I/O devices in parallel computing systems to enable communication and data exchange.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between static and dynamic networks?
flashcards.back: Static networks have fixed connections between nodes (e.g., mesh, ring), while dynamic networks can be reconfigured (e.g., crossbar, multistage networks).
The maximum shortest path between any two nodes in the network, representing the worst-case communication delay.
The minimum bandwidth between two halves of the network, indicating the capacity for communication between partitions.

flashcards.front: Name three switching techniques used in interconnection networks.
flashcards.back: Circuit switching, packet switching, and wormhole routing.
